input.command:
python3 -c "
import json
with open('articles/2026/03/21/articles.jsonl') as f:
    for line in f:
        a = json.loads(line)
        title = (a.get('title','') or '').lower()
        content = (a.get('content','') or '').lower()[:5000]
        for kw in ['ceasefire iran', 'trump pause iran', 'iran deal', 'oman iran', '15-point', 'trump plan iran', '10-day pause', 'iran ceasefire']:
            if kw in title or kw in content[:2000]:
                print(f'[{kw}] {a.get(\"title\",\"\")[:160]}')
                print(a.get('content','')[:500])
                print('---')
                break
" 2>/dev/null | head -30

blocks.content:
[bbc journalist] Long-serving BBC Journalist Jenni Murray Dies at 75
Jenni Murray, the English journalist who hosted BBC Radio 4’s Woman’s Hour for 33 years, died on March 12, age 75. Murray, who led the show from 1987 until 2020, was described as “a broadcasting icon,” by the outgoing BBC Director General Tim Davie. BBC Radio 4 controller Mohit Bakaya added that Murray “helped shape the national conversation with intelligence, rigor and a remarkable ability to connect with audiences.” Murray, who was made a Dame in 2011, interviewed the likes of Margaret Thatche
---

python3 -c "
import json
with open('articles/2026/03/21/articles.jsonl') as f:
    for line in f:
        a = json.loads(line)
        title = (a.get('title','') or '').lower()
        content = (a.get('content','') or '').lower()[:5000]
        for kw in ['trump intermediary', 'trump oman', 'trump qatar', 'convey iran', 'trump 15 point', 'fifteen-point']:
            if kw in title or kw in content[:2000]:
                print(f'[{kw}] {a.get(\"title\",\"\")[:160]}')
                print(a.get('content','')[:500])
                print('---')
                break
" 2>/dev/null | head -20
